idiom.label: V-E w/ V6/4 btw. I and I6 (or i and i6)
V-E w/ vii()6 btw. I and I6 (or i and i6)
|| 10ths w/ vii()6 btw. I and I6 (or i and i6)
V6/5 to I (or i) supporting 4^ - 3^ in sop
V4/3 to I or I6 (or i or i6), || 10ths
V4/2 to I6 (or i6) w/ rising fourth in sop
|| 10ths w/ V4/3 btw. I and I6 (or i and i6)

topic.label: The ii chord, esp. vs. IV

idiom.label: V-E w/ ii-vii()6 btw. I and I6
passing 6/4 expanding I or i w V-E
passing 6/4 expanding IV or iv w V-E
passing 6/3 expanding I or i w V-E
DP segment starts w. vi or VI, then has bass and root motion down by 3rds
DP segment starts w. vi or VI, then has root motion down by 5ths
descending l.t. or subtonic supported by iii or III
DP starts with iii or III, then has root motion down by 5ths
V4/2 to iii (or III) supporting rising fourth
circle of fifths seq., desc., all root pos.
circle of fifths seq., desc., alternating root pos. and 1st inv.
circle of fifths seq., asc., all root pos. (seq. breaks bef. cadence)
5-6 seq., desc., alternting root pos. and 1st inv.
5-6 seq., asc., alternating root pos. and 1st inv.
plagal cadence w. ii6/5 or ii(/)6/5
circle of fifths seq., desc., w/ 7th chords
DP segment starts w/ vi7 or VI7, then 7th chords w/ roots desc. by 5th
DP segment starts w/ iii7 or III7, then 7th chords w/ roots desc. by 5th
V-E expanding IV or iv w/ 2ndary V6/4
V-E expanding IV or iv w/ 2ndary vii()6/3
bVI adds a seventh and becomes Ger+6/5
